content:
type: DEFER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: Modify Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds) so that when merging a key whose existing value in self and the incoming value are BOTH dict instances (this includes Features objects and dict subclasses used inside Sequence(feature=...) since Features/Sequence.feature can hold nested dicts), the merge is a recursive deep-merge instead of a plain overwrite. Behavior needed: 1) Iterate items from  (supporting both a mapping via .keys()/[] and an iterable of (key, value) pairs, as today) and then items from **kwds, in that order (kwds applied after iterable, matching current order). 2) For each (key, value): if key in self and isinstance(self[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict), recursively deep-merge value into self[key] in place (i.e. self[key].update(value) recursively applied at every nesting level: for each subkey, if both sides are dicts recurse, else overwrite) rather than replacing self[key] outright. 3) If value is a Sequence(feature=...) or the existing self[key] is a Sequence whose .feature is a dict and the incoming value is also a Sequence-like with a dict .feature, the .feature dicts should be deep-merged the same way (so nested Sequence(feature={...}) definitions merge instead of being replaced) — simplest correct approach: write a small recursive helper, e.g.  that mutates dst in place: for k, v in src.items(): if k in dst and isinstance(dst[k], dict) and isinstance(v, dict): _deep_merge(dst[k], v) else: dst[k] = v. Then in update(), when both self[key] and value are plain dicts, call self[key] update via this helper instead of ; otherwise keep existing assignment behavior (self[key] = value) unchanged, including whatever __setitem__ instrumentation/decorator you add for _column_requires_decoding syncing. 4) This must not break your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator — the deep-merge should happen as part of update()'s body (before/instead of the plain assignment), and __setitem__ should still be invoked for keys that are newly added or non-dict-merged so your sync logic still runs. Unrelated keys not present in both are added intact as before.
note: deep-merge Features.update() so nested dict/Sequence(feature=dict) values merge recursively instead of overwriting; please implement alongside your decorator work since both touch update()

content:
type: SPEC
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: Modify Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds) so that when merging a key whose existing value in self and the incoming value are BOTH dict instances (this includes Features objects, and dict-based .feature attributes inside Sequence objects), the merge is a recursive deep-merge instead of a plain overwrite.

Behavior needed:
1) Iterate items from iterable (support both a mapping via .keys()/getitem and an iterable of (key, value) pairs, as today), then items from kwds, in that order (kwds applied after iterable, matching current order).
2) For each (key, value): if key in self and isinstance(self[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict): recursively deep-merge value into self[key] in place, instead of replacing self[key] outright. Otherwise: self[key] = value (unchanged behavior).
3) Add a small recursive helper function, e.g. named deep_merge_dicts(dst, src), that mutates dst in place: for each k, v in src.items(): if k in dst and isinstance(dst[k], dict) and isinstance(v, dict): recurse into dst[k], v; else: dst[k] = v.
4) When self[key] is a dict and gets deep-merged, still go through self[key] = <merged result> or an equivalent path so your setitem/decorator instrumentation for _column_requires_decoding still runs for that key (needed since merging can change whether the value requires decoding).
5) Unrelated keys not present in both are added intact as before, via plain assignment.

This should not conflict with your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator - the deep merge logic replaces only the inner assignment behavior of update(), not the method wrapping/decoration itself.
note: Resending clean version - my earlier DEFER message was corrupted by shell backtick expansion errors. This is the accurate spec for deep-merge in update().

fields.spec:
Modify Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds) so that when merging a key whose existing value in self and the incoming value are BOTH dict instances (this includes Features objects, and dict-based .feature attributes inside Sequence objects), the merge is a recursive deep-merge instead of a plain overwrite.

Behavior needed:
1) Iterate items from iterable (support both a mapping via .keys()/getitem and an iterable of (key, value) pairs, as today), then items from kwds, in that order (kwds applied after iterable, matching current order).
2) For each (key, value): if key in self and isinstance(self[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict): recursively deep-merge value into self[key] in place, instead of replacing self[key] outright. Otherwise: self[key] = value (unchanged behavior).
3) Add a small recursive helper function, e.g. named deep_merge_dicts(dst, src), that mutates dst in place: for each k, v in src.items(): if k in dst and isinstance(dst[k], dict) and isinstance(v, dict): recurse into dst[k], v; else: dst[k] = v.
4) When self[key] is a dict and gets deep-merged, still go through self[key] = <merged result> or an equivalent path so your setitem/decorator instrumentation for _column_requires_decoding still runs for that key (needed since merging can change whether the value requires decoding).
5) Unrelated keys not present in both are added intact as before, via plain assignment.

This should not conflict with your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator - the deep merge logic replaces only the inner assignment behavior of update(), not the method wrapping/decoration itself.
